skill.name: dpi-upscaler-checker
skill.description: Check if images meet 300 DPI printing standards, and intelligently restore blurry low-resolution images using AI super-resolution technology.
skill.summary: AI-powered image DPI validation and super-resolution upscaling for print-ready output.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 55 static analysis findings are false positives. The 12 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are argparse code, markdown tables, and JSON data misidentified as crypto. The 39 'shell backtick execution' marks are CLI usage examples in SKILL.md code blocks, which is the documented way the AI agent invokes this skill. The 2 filesystem operations are legitimate report generation and output directory creation. Two bare except: blocks were found at main.py:100 and main.py:248 that the static scanner missed. The skill is well-documented, transparent, and performs exactly as described with no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Bare except clause catches all exceptions
low_findings.description: Two bare except: blocks in main.py suppress all exceptions including KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit. These should specify Exception type for safety.

content.user_title: Check and Upscale Images to 300 DPI Print Standards
content.value_statement: Print shops and publishers often reject images that do not meet 300 DPI standards. This skill checks image DPI requirements and restores low-resolution images using AI super-resolution technology.
actual_capabilities: Check single images or entire folders for 300 DPI compliance
Generate detailed DPI reports with print size calculations
Upscale low-resolution images using AI super-resolution (Real-ESRGAN, OpenCV, or PIL)
Batch process folders of images for print readiness
Preserve EXIF metadata in processed images
limitations: Super-resolution cannot create details that do not exist in the original image
GPU acceleration requires a CUDA-compatible environment to be installed
Batch upscale mode does not generate a JSON summary report
Extremely blurry or heavily compressed images show limited improvement

use_cases.title: Print Shop Quality Control
use_cases.description: A print shop operator checks customer images for 300 DPI compliance before printing.
use_cases.target_user: Print shop operators and prepress technicians

use_cases.title: Scientific Image Preparation
use_cases.description: A researcher upscales low-resolution microscopy images to meet journal publication DPI requirements.

use_cases.title: Digital Asset Management
use_cases.description: A designer batch-processes a folder of legacy images to bring them up to print-ready resolution.
use_cases.target_user: Graphic designers and digital asset managers

prompt_templates.title: Quick DPI Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if image.jpg meets 300 DPI standards.
prompt_templates.scenario: New user checking a single image

prompt_templates.title: Batch DPI Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Check all images in the ./photos/ folder and save a JSON report to ./report.json.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to audit multiple images

prompt_templates.title: Single Image Upscale
prompt_templates.prompt: Upscale image.jpg by 4x and save to upscaled.jpg. Set output DPI to 300.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to improve a specific low-resolution image

prompt_templates.title: Batch Fix Low DPI Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Process all images in ./input/ that are below 300 DPI, upscale by 2x, and save to ./output/.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user fixing a folder of images for print

output_examples.output: photo.jpg has 72 DPI and does not meet the 300 DPI print standard. Recommended upscale factor: 4.17x.
Processing successful. Size went from 640x480 to 2560x1920 pixels. Image saved to blurry_image_upscaled.jpg at 300 DPI.
Checked 15 images: 3 meet 300 DPI, 10 need upscaling, 2 had errors. Full report saved to report.json.

best_practices: Always run a DPI check first before upscaling to determine the required scale factor
Use the minimum effective scale factor to avoid unnecessary file size increases
Enable face enhancement for images containing people for better results
anti_patterns: Upscaling images that already meet 300 DPI standards wastes processing time and disk space
Using maximum scale factor on every image without checking actual needs creates oversized files
Processing images without specifying an output directory can lose results in batch mode

faq.question: What is 300 DPI and why is it important?
faq.answer: 300 DPI (dots per inch) is the standard print resolution. Images below this threshold appear pixelated when printed.

faq.question: Can this skill improve any blurry image?
faq.answer: No. Super-resolution cannot create information that does not exist. Very blurry images show limited improvement.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: JPG, PNG, TIFF, BMP, and WebP formats are supported for both checking and upscaling.

faq.question: Do I need a GPU to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill works on CPU. GPU acceleration (CUDA) is optional and provides faster processing.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my original files?
faq.answer: No. Upscaled images are saved as new files with _upscaled suffix. Original files are never modified.

faq.question: What happens during batch processing?
faq.answer: The skill scans all supported images in the folder, checks each one, and processes only those that need improvement.
